skill.name: just-scrape
skill.description: Search, scrape, crawl, extract structured data, and monitor web pages via the ScrapeGraph AI CLI. Use when the user asks to search the web, scrape a webpage, grab content from a URL, extract JSON from a site, crawl documentation or site sections, monitor a page for changes, inspect request history, check ScrapeGraph credits, or validate API setup.
skill.summary: Web search, scraping, crawling, and AI-powered data extraction via the ScrapeGraph CLI
skill.author: scrapegraphai

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 175 external command patterns (all legitimate bunx/npm invocations) plus network, filesystem, and env access. The skill is a web scraping CLI wrapper that executes trusted tooling. All patterns are benign use cases for scraping workflows. Risk level is elevated to medium due to the inherent nature of external command execution in a scraping context, but this is appropriate for the skill's intended purpose.

medium_findings.title: External Command Execution for CLI Invocation
medium_findings.description: The skill executes external CLI commands via bunx/npm. This is inherent to the skill's design as a CLI wrapper tool. Commands are hardcoded invocations of the just-scrape CLI with structured arguments, not user-controlled input.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Pattern found in documentation but represents legitimate CLI invocation, not code injection. Arguments are structured and controlled.

medium_findings.title: Network Access for Web Scraping
medium_findings.description: The skill makes network requests to scrape web content. This is the core purpose of the skill. Network activity is scoped to user-provided URLs or known ScrapeGraph endpoints.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Network access is the intended functionality. URLs are user-provided for scraping targets.

medium_findings.title: API Key Access for ScrapeGraph Authentication
medium_findings.description: The skill requires SGAI_API_KEY for authentication. Environment variable access is standard for API key management and is explicitly documented as the correct approach.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: API key access is intentional and properly documented. Keys are read from environment variables, not hardcoded.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Reference (Documentation)
low_findings.description: SKILL.md line 3 contains base64 encoding documentation. This is standard data encoding, not cryptography. The heuristic flag is a false positive.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: base64 is data encoding, not weak cryptography. Documentation reference is appropriate for data handling patterns.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance in CLI Help
low_findings.description: SKILL.md contains CLI help output showing system commands. This is documentation of the tool's capabilities, not actual reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: CLI command documentation does not constitute actual system reconnaissance. Commands are tool features, not system probing.

low_findings.title: Hidden File Access in Configuration
low_findings.description: SKILL.md references ~/.scrapegraphai/config.json. This is a standard configuration file location used by the tool.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Standard configuration file path. Access is for authentication setup, not data exfiltration.

content.user_title: Extract web content with AI-powered scraping
content.value_statement: Researchers and developers need to gather structured data from websites but face challenges with anti-scraping measures and complex page structures. just-scrape provides a CLI tool that uses AI to intelligently extract content from web pages.
actual_capabilities: Search the web for topics and return structured results with provider names and prices
Scrape web pages and extract content in multiple formats (markdown, HTML, screenshots, JSON)
Use AI-powered extraction with custom prompts to pull structured data from any URL
Crawl entire website sections with configurable depth and page limits
Monitor web pages for changes on a schedule and send webhook notifications
Track credit usage and validate ScrapeGraph API authentication
limitations: Requires a ScrapeGraph API key and credits for all operations
Cannot bypass all anti-bot protections; JS rendering mode may be needed
Large-scale crawling may consume significant credits quickly

use_cases.title: Research data collection
use_cases.description: Gather structured data from multiple sources for research papers, market analysis, or competitive intelligence. Extract pricing tables, product listings, or article metadata.

use_cases.title: Documentation and content aggregation
use_cases.description: Crawl documentation sites or knowledge bases to build local reference materials. Monitor pages for updates to keep content fresh.

use_cases.title: Price and availability monitoring
use_cases.description: Track product pages or listings for price changes and availability updates. Receive notifications when tracked pages change.

prompt_templates.title: Basic web search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for information about {topic} and tell me the top results.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to find web sources on a topic without knowing specific URLs

prompt_templates.title: Extract structured data from URL
prompt_templates.prompt: Scrape the content from {url} and extract {what_to_extract}.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a specific URL and knows what data they want

prompt_templates.title: Crawl documentation section
prompt_templates.prompt: Crawl the documentation at {url} up to {max_pages} pages and save all content in markdown format.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to build a local copy of documentation

prompt_templates.title: Monitor page for changes
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up a monitor to check {url} every {interval} and notify me if the content changes.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants ongoing tracking of a page for updates

output_examples.output: Extracted markdown content from the page showing headings, paragraphs, and links in readable format.

output_examples.input: just-scrape extract https://news.example.com -p "Extract all article titles, dates, and author names"
output_examples.output: Structured JSON with array of articles containing title, date, and author fields for each story.

best_practices: Check your credit balance with just-scrape credits before running bulk operations to avoid unexpected costs
Use --max-pages and --max-depth limits when crawling to control scope and credit usage
Store results in the .just-scrape directory and add it to .gitignore to keep your workspace organized
anti_patterns: Do not run unbounded crawls without setting limits; this can consume all your credits quickly
Avoid re-scraping URLs you just crawled; use the --json output for further processing
Do not pass raw scraped content directly into prompts without marking it as untrusted input

faq.question: How do I get started with just-scrape?
faq.answer: Install the CLI with npm install -g just-scrape, then set your SGAI_API_KEY environment variable. Run just-scrape validate to verify your setup.

faq.question: Why am I getting authentication errors?
faq.answer: Make sure SGAI_API_KEY is set correctly. You can set it in a .env file, ~/.scrapegraphai/config.json, or export it directly. Run just-scrape validate to check.

faq.question: How much will scraping cost?
faq.answer: Each operation consumes ScrapeGraph credits. Check your balance with just-scrape credits. Stealth mode, JS rendering, and crawling many pages use more credits.

faq.question: Why is my scrape returning empty or incomplete content?
faq.answer: Try adding --mode js for JavaScript-rendered pages, or --stealth and --scrolls for pages with lazy loading. Check if the site blocks scraping.

faq.question: How do I extract data in a specific format?
faq.answer: Use the --schema flag to specify a JSON schema for strict output shape. Or use -p with a prompt to guide the AI extraction.

faq.question: Can I monitor pages for changes automatically?
faq.answer: Yes, use just-scrape monitor create with --interval and optionally --webhook-url for notifications. Check monitors with just-scrape monitor list.
